title: Build a nightly observe-and-diagnose cycle
goal: Run a bounded nightly cycle that observes repository and runtime health, quick validation, RepoBrief freshness, task supply and recurring friction, then produces a source-bound morning digest without mutating repositories or creating tasks automatically.

approval.note: This task implements the cycle in repositories. The resulting production cycle is constrained to read-only observation and requires a separate plan for later effects.

acceptance.assertion: The cycle uses an explicit repository and service allowlist, resource budgets, per-probe timeouts and a global runtime ceiling.

The digest covers source commit and dirty state, quick validation receipt, open PR and CI state where available, RepoBrief freshness, failed services or tasks, recovery-gate state, ready-task supply and recurring friction classifications.
The first production version performs no pull, checkout, patch, push, merge, deploy, cleanup, service restart, queue mutation or automatic task creation.
Every finding carries source, timestamp, freshness and confidence; unknown or unavailable probes remain explicit instead of becoming green.
Unchanged findings are deduplicated, severity and recurrence thresholds are defined, and the morning digest remains bounded and operator-readable.
One failed repository or connector probe cannot abort the complete cycle; failures are isolated, recorded and retried only under the bounded transport policy.

lease_scope_migration.rationale: The nightly cycle requires bounded observation code, scheduled execution, diagnostic state and its no-mutation contract.
does_not_establish: safe mutation of terminal historical tasks
